evals.prompt: The text is overflowing the cards in the workshop deck my Python script builds with python-pptx. Sort it out and regenerate the deck.
evals.expected_output: The generator is reworked to bake explicit font sizes via the fit_size helper with no autofit anywhere, then the deck is rendered to images and swept for overflow before being declared done.
expectations: Grepping the generator for TEXT_TO_FIT_SHAPE, normAutofit, and auto_size returns nothing.
Every wrapped body text box gets an explicit Pt(size) whose value comes from fit_size or an equivalent baked size, not autofit.
The deck was rendered pptx -> pdf -> images (soffice + pdftoppm) and an overflow sweep was run across all slides.
The overflow sweep reports zero clipped, overlapping, or footer-colliding text boxes.

evals.prompt: I regenerated the deck and the PDF preview looks clean. Is it good to send?
evals.expected_output: The agent refuses to certify the deck on a LibreOffice/PDF preview while autofit may remain, explaining that LibreOffice hides the overflow that PowerPoint shows, and only trusts the preview after confirming the generator is autofit-free.
expectations: The reasoning states that LibreOffice recomputes the autofit scale but PowerPoint and Keynote do not.
The agent does NOT declare the deck correct from that preview while any autofit remains in the generator.
A preview is treated as faithful only after the generator is confirmed free of autofit.

evals.prompt: Build the slide deck for this methodology from the YAML in the project.
evals.expected_output: The generator sources palette, fonts, wordmark and layout from the project's current brand template rather than inventing them, and the output is checked against the template.
expectations: The palette (hex), fonts and wordmark are sourced from the project's current brand template (resolved by listing the brand folder), not invented or remembered.
Every brand colour and font in the generator traces to that template or guidelines at a stated version, with no approximated values.
If no brand template is findable, the agent asks which brand applies rather than guessing a palette.
The render sweep checks palette, fonts and wordmark against the template and treats a wrong colour or a substituted font as a defect.

evals.prompt: I'm tidying up scripts/fit_text.py for the deck generators. Review the fit_size helper.
evals.expected_output: The review confirms fit_size's contract and preserves the load-bearing calibration constants, rather than 'simplifying' them.
expectations: fit_size returns max_size unchanged when the text already fits the box.
fit_size returns a smaller size for text that overflows at max_size.
CHAR_W == 0.66 and SAFETY == 0.94 are preserved, or a re-calibration against rendered output is documented in the change.

evals.prompt: Pull the text out of /path/to/board-deck.pptx into a one-page summary for me.
evals.expected_output: The skill does not fire; extraction is handled by the built-in pptx skill and no generator or autofit changes are proposed.
expectations: No autofit, fit_size, or generator changes are proposed.
The agent defers to the built-in pptx skill for extraction (and would treat Canva / Google Slides / HTML decks as out of scope).
